data.title: Apple to analyze user data to train AI models
data.description:
Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) will start analyzing user data stored on devices to help train its artificial intelligence models, the company said on Monday.

Shares rose 3.5% in midday trading. 

The user data will add to the synthetic data that Apple already uses for its AI models. However, it will protect user's privacy by ensuring that the data remains on devices and is not used to directly train the models. This could allow it to catch up to competitors, such as OpenAI, who incorporate real-world data from various sources to train their models.

The change in models will allow Apple to take the synthetic data it has created and compared it to a sample of user emails inside the email app on iPhones, iPads and Macs. Apple can then test the synthetic data against the actual emails to determine which synthetic data inputs are most accurate. 

“When creating synthetic data, our goal is to produce synthetic sentences or emails that are similar enough in topic or style to the real thing to help improve our models for summarization, but without Apple collecting emails from the device,” Apple wrote on its website. 

Apple said last month that it had delayed a more personalized version of its Siri digital assistant.

data.title: VeeMost Technologies, formerly Global Developments, announces rebranding
data.description:
IT solutions provider VeeMost Technologies Holdings (OTCPK:GDVM) announced on Monday a change of name from Global Developments, and a change in the ticker symbol to VMST, effective open of business on April 15.

The company is also changing the CUSIP number to 922462106.

data.title: Seeking Alpha's top Quant picks in Utilities sector as Q1 earnings approaches
data.description:
The S&P 500's Utilities Sector (NYSEARCA:XLU) gained over 3% during the first quarter, compared to over 4% decline in the broader market.

The sector, which holds a 2.21% weightage on the S&P 500 Index, rose 2.42% so far in 2025, outperforming the broader market index, which declined nearly 9% during the same period.

According to the latest data from Seeking Alpha's Quant Rating System, stocks making up the utilities index have secured an average health score of 4.32. The system awards grades based on quantitative measures, like valuation, earnings growth and recent stock performance. The highest possible score for any individual company is a 5.

Looking into individual companies in the sector, based on their quant ratings, three out of 31 stocks are rated as a Strong Buy and six stocks are rated as a Buy. While 20 stocks are rated Neutral, two of them are considered Sell.

Exelon (EXC) has the highest Quant score of 4.94, with revision and momentum boosting its ratings. The company’s stock skyrocketed over 22% in the first quarter.

NiSource (NI) took the second spot, with a score of 4.81, with the revision and momentum boosting its ratings.

Ameren (AEE) has the third-highest Quant score of 4.54, with momentum and EPS revision boosting its ratings. The company, which is expected to report first quarter earnings on May 2, gained nearly 13% in the first quarter.

Vistra (VST) has the lowest Quant score of 2.44 in the sector, with a D- grade in valuation and D grade in momentum and revisions sinking its ratings.

According to a FactSet report, the estimated Q1 earnings growth rate for the S&P 500 is 10% year-over-year, with the utilities sector expected to report the third-highest (year-over-year) earnings growth rate of all eleven sectors at 10.4%, the report said.

At the industry level, all 5 industries in the sector are projected to report year-over-year earnings growth, with Independent Power and Renewable Energy Producers (138%), Water Utilities (13%), multi-utilities (10%), Gas Utilities (7%), and Electric Utilities (7%), noted the report.

Seeking Alpha analyst Harrison Schwartz noted that XLU is among the few sector ETFs that offer defensive potential in the current market environment with both inflation resurgence and cyclical risk factors.

However, Schwartz cautioned that growing bipartisan antitrust sentiment and utility lobbying regulations may hamper utilities' ability to raise rates in the long term.

data.title: Goldman cites a “positive surprise” for GSK ahead of ACIP meeting on RSV shot
data.description:
Goldman Sachs said Monday that GSK (NYSE:GSK) is set for a 'positive surprise' this week as a CDC Advisory Committee is scheduled to discuss whether to broaden the recommendation for Arexvy, the British drugmaker’s vaccine for respiratory syncytial virus (RSV).

Its remarks came ahead of a meeting of CDC's Advisory Committee on Immunization Practices (ACIP), which is tasked with endorsing Arexvy and Pfizer’s (NYSE:PFE) rival shot Abrysvo for a younger cohort of older Americans.

The CDC currently recommends Arexvy and Abrysvo for Americans aged 60-74 who are at an increased risk of severe RSV, and for everyone over 70 years.

After the ACIP meeting on Wednesday, ACIP panelists will vote on whether to recommend a single RSV shot for adults 50–59 years of age who are at increased risk of severe RSV disease.

Citing a CDC presentation  published before the meeting, Goldman analyst Rajan Sharma argued that “ACIP voting to expand the recommended population for Arexvy to those aged 50–59 ‘at-risk’ is likely, which would represent a positive surprise for GSK, in our view.”

While Moderna (MRNA) has an FDA-approved RSV vaccine, its messenger-RNA-based shot is only approved by the FDA for those aged 60 years and older.

CDC advisory committees issue non-binding recommendations. However, the federal agency usually follows them before issuing its final guidelines.

data.title: TC Energy finishes pipeline repairs in Michigan following gas leak
data.description:
TC Energy (NYSE:TRP) completed repairs to a section of the ANR Pipeline system near Fennville, Michigan, that was shut down last week because of a leak, Reuters reported Monday.

TC Energy (TRP) isolated and shut the damaged part of the pipeline after it was struck by a third party, causing an outage that affected gas supplies to ~5,200 homes in the area.

TC Energy's ANR pipeline system spans 9,367 miles, transporting more than 10B cf/day of natural gas to U.S. Midwestern states.

data.title: Philip Morris earnings preview: Tobacco's big winner is expected to keep rallying
data.description:
Tobacco stocks have been viewed favorably by investors over the last few months due to the limited exposure to tariffs and lackluster outlooks in other consumer sectors. Philip Morris International (NYSE:PM) has been the big winner in the tobacco sector, with a +30% year-to-date rally amid market chaos.

Looking ahead, Bank of America said it still sees Philip Morris National (NYSE:PM) as an attractive defense name with growth appeal. The firm upped its price target on Philip Morris International (PM) to $175, which values the tobacco stock at 22X the 2026 EPS of $7.98. Analyst Lisa Lewandowski said that while the price-to-earnings multiple on Philip Morris (PM) has rerated and is now closer to PEs of mega cap consumer peers Procter & Gamble (PG), Coca-Cola (KO), and Mondelez International (MDLZ), the firm thinks the premium is warranted due to the strength of PM's operations and the positive outlook. 

"We think mgmt guidance updates and insights on consumer reactions to changing economic conditions will be a key focus of this earnings season," wrote Lewandowski.

Meanwhile, Morgan Stanley views Philip Morris (PM) as an outlier in a scarce growth CPG environment that also has a defensive portfolio and benefits from a weaker U.S. dollar. "We expect robust PM smoke-free growth along with modest growth in combustible sales/profit to enable PMI to deliver on the upper half of its 6-8% OSG, and above its 8-10% OI, and 9-11% FX-N EPS algorithm through 2026, the highest among mega-cap CPG peers," highlighted analyst Dara Mohsenian.

In terms of quantitative analysis, Philip Morris (PM) ranks in the top 4% of all consumer staples names and has a Seeking Alpha Quant Rating of Strong Buy. Wall Street analysts and Seeking Alpha analysts both have a Buy rating on PM as well.

Shares of Philip Morris International (PM) were up 2.6% in afternoon trading on Monday and are more than 75% over the last 52 weeks. The dividend yield for new buyers of the stock is 3.51%.

Philip Morris (PM) is scheduled to report Q1 earnings on April 23. The Stamford, Connecticut company is expected to report revenue of $9.12 billion and EPS of $1.61.

data.title: Comcast gets downgraded at Wells Fargo on C&P woes ahead
data.description:
Analysts at Wells Fargo downgraded media and cable giant Comcast (NASDAQ:CMCSA) to "underweight" from a previous investment rating of "equal weight."

The research firm thinks the stock is no longer defensive as it is being pulled into a convergence investment cycle with higher mobile costs to re-accelerate broadband.

They see Connectivity & Platforms to remain pressured and estimate EBITDA growth in the segment for '25/'26 of -1.7%/-0.2% y/y and net adds of -500k/-420k.

"While FWA net adds are expected to be flat this year, we think the fiber headwind is still increasing due to the cumulative effect of ~5 years of aggressive overbuilding and a push to increase penetration faster," Wells Fargo said Monday.

"Connectivity & Platforms is the lion's share of CMCSA's earnings, and it faces historically intense competition from fiber overbuilding. In response, we expect CMCSA to invest into mobile+b'band convergence packaging, which lowers growth and requires time/risk to pay off in subscriber trends," the research firm added.

They also see an impact on NBCU due to a weakened ad market, expect elevated sports costs for the NBA, and see no break-even at Peacock.

CMCSA's price target was cut by $6 to $31, implying a downside of 8.1%.

data.title: Fed could cut rates later this year under 'smaller tariff' scenario, Fed Governor Waller says
data.description:
The tariff situation is changing quickly, and recent moves only complicate the picture, Federal Reserve Governor Chris Waller said on Monday. Still, he sees the potential for a path that could lead to the Federal Open Market Committee to cut rates later this year.

"Considering all this uncertainty, it is impossible to forecast how the economy will evolve very far into the future," he said at the Certified Financial Analysts Society of St. Louis.

When faced with such a fluid environment, Waller said he prefers to lay out possible tariff scenarios to decide how to approach monetary policy in the coming months.

In one scenario, he estimates that tariffs will remain very high and be long-lasting, near the current average rate of 25% or more, as compared with the average rate of just under 3% early in 2025. In a second scenario, trade negotiations lead to the removal of most of the new tariffs, reducing the average tariff rate to ~10%.

Even in the larger tariff scenario, Waller sees only a temporary effect on inflation. Still, even if the tariffs were only partially passed on to the consumer, inflation could increase to ~4%, he said.

"It will be important to watch inflation expectations and make sure they remain anchored during this process," he said. 

Other factors may limit the increase in inflation. Monetary policy is still "meaningfully" restrictive, and he hopes that underlying inflation may moderate over the course of the year, separate from tariff effects. And if the economy slows substantially, "then weaker demand will put downward pressure on inflation after tariffs take effect."

"In terms of output growth, with large tariff increases, I would expect the U.S. economy to slow significantly later this year and this slower pace to continue into next year," Waller said.

He expects that the peak effect from the smaller tariff scenario would be ~3% on an annualized basis. "Since it may take some time for tariff-related price increases to work their way through production chains, the peak may be lower but still dissipate slowly," he said.

"As a result of these limited effects on inflation and economic activity from steadily diminishing tariffs, I would support a limited monetary policy response," Waller said.

With anchored or possibly lower inflation expectations amid slower economic growth, along with the view that the smaller tariff effects are temporary, "gives the FOMC room to adjust policy as progress on the underlying trend in inflation is revealed in price data," he explained. As such, he believes the threat of a sharp slowdown or recession diminishing would also reduce pressure to cut rates. That scenario could allow for even more patience in monetary policy.

If that plays out, "I would expect inflation to continue on its path down towards our 2 percent target. In this case, 'good news' rate cuts are very much on the table in the latter half of this year," he said.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Orla Mining rises after reaching record gold production in Q1
data.description:
Orla Mining (NYSE:ORLA) +3.7% in Monday's trading after saying it achieved record quarterly gold production of 47,759 oz in Q1, placing it on a path to meeting full-year guidance of 110K-120K oz.

Orla (NYSE:ORLA) said it produced 29,973 oz at the Camino Rojo mine in Mexico while stacking more than 1.67M metric tons of ore, with a daily stacking rate of 18.4K tons/day at an average grade of 0.78 g/t gold, in line with expectations, while the Musselwhite operation in Ontario during March alone produced 17,786 oz, milling 104K tons at a mill head grade of 5.55 g/t gold, yielding a 95.7% recovery rate.

Orla (ORLA) completed its acquisition of the Musselwhite operation from Newmont at the beginning of March, and earlier this month announced the start of the 2025 exploration drilling program at the mine.

The company said it plans to complete ~35K meters of underground drilling this year to offset mine depletion and grow reserves, resources and mineral inventories, after producing nearly 6M oz of gold over its 28-year operating history.

data.title: Nasdaq, S&P, and Dow rise as Trump spares tech sector from retaliatory tariffs
data.description:
Traders and investors watch the major market averages rise on Monday as President Donald Trump announced temporary tariff exemptions on smartphones, computers, and other electronics primarily imported from China.

The tech focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was +0.9%, the benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was 1%, and the blue-chip Dow (DJI) was +1%. 

On a sector-by-sector basis, nine of the 11 S&P sectors have moved up into positive territory with Utilities and Real Estate leading the way. At the other end, Consumer Discretionary has been the weakest performer on the day. 

Shifting to the Treasury market and yields are lower. The longer end U.S. 10 Year Treasury yield (US10Y) fell 12 basis points to 4.37%. At the same time, the shorter end U.S. 2 Year Treasury yield (US2Y) fell 13 basis points to 3.84%. 

Wall Street’s positive momentum push has been fueled by Trump's announcement that tariffs were exempt for smartphones, computers, and other electronics mostly imported from China, providing major relief for American tech players. 

"Tariffs continued to dominate headlines over the weekend, starting with news late on Friday that smartphones, computers, and some other electronics would be exempt from U.S. reciprocal tariffs," Deutsche Bank's Peter Sidorov said. 

"Over the weekend, Trump partially retreated from the retreat, emphasizing that a 20% tax would apply and additional unspecified taxes on technology are coming. This erratic policy making leads to investors questioning the existence of a competent plan," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

The upcoming week is set to feature a wave of major earnings reports from top companies, including Bank of America (BAC), Netflix (NFLX), Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSM), Johnson & Johnson (JNJ), and American Express (AXP).

On the economic front, U.S. consumers have slightly tempered their inflation expectations for the long-term even as they hike their projections on short-term inflation, according to the Federal Reserve Bank of New York's March Survey of Consumer Expectations released on Monday.

Additionally, Atlanta Fed President Raphael Bostic and Richmond Fed President Thomas Barkin make remarks.

As for stocks that are on the move, Palantir pops 4% after landing a deal to supply NATO with AI-powered battle system.
